rag.description: InvokeUser32DoubleClick performs Windows User32 API double-click actions on native desktop applications. It supports coordinate-based double-clicking at absolute viewport positions and element-targeted double-clicking with configurable alignment and pixel offsets. Requires a User32-compatible WebDriver and runs exclusively on Windows.

qa.question: What does InvokeUser32DoubleClick do?
qa.answer: It performs double-click actions using the Windows User32 API, supporting absolute coordinate double-clicks and element-targeted double-clicks with configurable alignment and pixel offsets.

qa.question: What parameters does InvokeUser32DoubleClick support?
qa.answer: X and Y for absolute viewport coordinates; OffsetX and OffsetY for pixel offsets relative to the target element; Alignment to set the double-click reference point on the element (default: MiddleCenter).

qa.question: When is coordinate mode used instead of element mode?
qa.answer: Coordinate mode activates when at least one of X or Y is non-zero and no target element is provided. Element mode is used when an element is targeted via OnElement.

qa.question: How does InvokeUser32DoubleClick integrate with sequential workflows?
qa.answer: It runs as a task-type action in the G4 sequential workflow model, targeting native Windows UI elements through a User32-compatible WebDriver.

qa.question: What locator strategy does InvokeUser32DoubleClick support?
qa.answer: Only Xpath is supported for locating User32 elements.

qa.question: What happens when the WebDriver does not support IUser32Driver?
qa.answer: The plugin returns an empty response immediately and no action is performed.

description: ### Purpose
The primary purpose of the `InvokeUser32DoubleClick` plugin is to perform double-click actions using the Windows User32 API.
Automation scripts can interact with native UI elements in desktop applications by double-clicking at specific coordinates or on a target element with configurable offsets and alignment.
### Key Features and Functionality
| Feature                          | Description                                                                                                     |
| Native Double-Click Action       | Executes double-click actions using the User32 API for native Windows UI elements.                              |
| Coordinate-based Double-Clicking | Supports double-clicking at specific (X, Y) coordinates when no target element is provided.                     |
| Element-specific Double-Clicking | Moves the mouse to a target element with configurable offsets and alignment before performing the double-click. |
### Usage in RPA
| Usage                | Description                                                                                                              |
| Desktop Automation   | Integrate with RPA workflows to perform double-click actions on native desktop UI elements using the Windows User32 API. |
| Flexible Interaction | Automate complex scenarios by specifying exact double-click coordinates or targeting specific UI elements with offsets.  |
### Usage in Automation Testing
| Usage              | Description                                                                                                                  |
| UI Testing         | Verify the functionality of native UI components by simulating double-click actions with configurable offsets and alignment. |
| Regression Testing | Ensure consistent behavior of UI elements after updates by automating double-click sequences using the User32 API.           |
### Platform
This plugin is designed to work on **Windows** only.

edge_cases: Both X and Y are zero — coordinate mode does not activate; element mode is used instead
Non-IUser32Driver detected — plugin returns an empty response with no action performed
Viewport coordinates outside the visible screen area may produce no visible double-click effect
Only one of X or Y is non-zero — coordinate mode still activates
annotations.expected_result: The mouse cursor moves to viewport coordinates (100, 200) and a double-click is performed.
annotations.notes: Coordinate mode activates when at least one of X or Y is non-zero and no element is targeted. The `{{$ --X:100 --Y:200}}` syntax binds X and Y as a parameters dictionary before execution.

description: ### Double-click at absolute viewport coordinates
The plugin moves the system mouse cursor to position (100, 200) on the viewport and performs a double-click.
No element is targeted; the `{{$ --X:100 --Y:200}}` syntax binds X and Y as a parameters dictionary that activates coordinate mode at runtime.

edge_cases: Element not found — an exception is raised by the User32 driver
Non-IUser32Driver detected — plugin returns an empty response with no action performed
Offset values push the double-click outside the element bounding box — the double-click still executes at the computed position
Both X and Y are zero with an element present — element mode activates normally
annotations.expected_result: The mouse moves to the TopLeft corner of the target button plus the specified offsets and a double-click is performed.
annotations.notes: Element mode activates when an element is provided and X and Y are both zero. Alignment and offset parameters adjust the final cursor position within or around the element bounding box.

description: ### Double-click on element with alignment and offset

The plugin locates the element at `//button[@id='SubmitButton']` using the Xpath locator, moves the mouse to the TopLeft alignment point with a 10-pixel horizontal and 20-pixel vertical offset, then performs a double-click.
The `{{$ --OffsetX:10 --OffsetY:20 --Alignment:TopLeft}}` syntax binds the parameters as a dictionary before execution.
Controls the alignment point on the target element where the mouse cursor is positioned before double-clicking.
Alignment determines which corner, edge, or center of the element bounding box serves as the double-click reference point.
Combine with OffsetX and OffsetY to fine-tune the double-click position relative to the chosen alignment point.
Use MiddleCenter for standard centered double-clicks or edge and corner values for precise boundary interactions.
parameters.name: Alignment

description: Positions the cursor at the bottom center of the element bounding box.
Use this alignment when the target action is near the lower middle edge of the element.
values.name: BottomCenter

description: Positions the cursor at the bottom left corner of the element bounding box.
Use this alignment when the target action is near the lower left boundary of the element.
values.name: BottomLeft

description: Positions the cursor at the bottom right corner of the element bounding box.
Use this alignment when the target action is near the lower right boundary of the element.
values.name: BottomRight

description: Positions the cursor at the horizontal and vertical center of the element bounding box.
This is the default alignment and works well for most standard double-click interactions.
values.name: MiddleCenter

description: Positions the cursor at the left edge and vertical center of the element bounding box.
Use this alignment when the target action is near the left side of the element.
values.name: MiddleLeft

description: Positions the cursor at the right edge and vertical center of the element bounding box.
Use this alignment when the target action is near the right side of the element.
values.name: MiddleRight

description: Positions the cursor at the top center of the element bounding box.
Use this alignment when the target action is near the upper middle edge of the element.
values.name: TopCenter

description: Positions the cursor at the top left corner of the element bounding box.
Use this alignment when the target action is near the upper left boundary of the element.
values.name: TopLeft

description: Positions the cursor at the top right corner of the element bounding box.
Use this alignment when the target action is near the upper right boundary of the element.
values.name: TopRight

description: Specifies the horizontal pixel offset applied to the mouse position relative to the aligned point on the target element.
Positive values shift the double-click position to the right; negative values shift it to the left.
Use this parameter when the double-click must land slightly away from the default alignment point.
parameters.name: OffsetX

description: Specifies the vertical pixel offset applied to the mouse position relative to the aligned point on the target element.
Positive values shift the double-click position downward; negative values shift it upward.
Use this parameter when the double-click must land slightly away from the default alignment point.
parameters.name: OffsetY

description: Specifies the absolute horizontal coordinate on the viewport where the double-click will occur when no target element is provided.
The coordinate is measured in pixels from the left edge of the viewport.
Set X and Y together to trigger coordinate-based double-clicking without element targeting.
parameters.name: X

description: Specifies the absolute vertical coordinate on the viewport where the double-click will occur when no target element is provided.
The coordinate is measured in pixels from the top edge of the viewport.
Set X and Y together to trigger coordinate-based double-clicking without element targeting.
parameters.name: Y

description: Carries the parameter expression used to pass runtime values to the plugin.
Use the `{{$ --Name:Value}}` format to specify OffsetX, OffsetY, Alignment, X, or Y at runtime.
The expression is parsed into a parameters dictionary before the plugin executes.
properties.name: Argument

description: Specifies the strategy used to locate the target UI element before performing the double-click.
Xpath is the only supported locator strategy for User32 elements in this plugin.
properties.name: Locator

description: Specifies the XPath expression that identifies the target UI element for the double-click action.
When provided, the plugin resolves the element, moves the mouse to the aligned position, and performs the double-click.
Omit this property to use coordinate-based double-clicking with the X and Y parameters instead.
properties.name: OnElement

summary: The `InvokeUser32DoubleClick` plugin performs double-click actions using the Windows User32 API on native desktop applications.
It supports coordinate-based double-clicks at absolute viewport positions and element-targeted double-clicks with configurable alignment and pixel offsets.
The plugin is exclusively for Windows platforms and requires a User32-compatible WebDriver.
